AddProjectsDirs adds additional root directories to scan for JSONL files.\n// Call this after NewDashboard to include directories beyond the default ~/.claude/projects.\nfunc (d *Dashboard) AddProjectsDirs(dirs []string) {\n\tfor _, dir := range dirs {\n\t\td.tokenCollector.AddProjectsDir(dir)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Init initializes the dashboard\nfunc (d *Dashboard) Init() tea.Cmd {\n\treturn tea.Batch(\n\t\td.tick(),\n\t\td.collectMetrics(),\n\t\td.checkForUpdates(),\n\t)\n}\n\n// updateCheckMsg carries update check results\ntype updateCheckMsg struct {\n\tinfo *updater.UpdateInfo\n}\n\n// updateCompleteMsg indicates update was applied\ntype updateCompleteMsg struct {\n\terr error\n}\n\n// checkForUpdates returns a command that checks for updates\nfunc (d *Dashboard) checkForUpdates() tea.Cmd {\n\treturn func() tea.Msg {\n\t\tinfo := d.updater.CheckForUpdate()\n\t\treturn updateCheckMsg{info: info}\n\t}\n}\n\n// Update handles messages\nfunc (d *Dashboard) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {\n\tswitch msg := msg.(type) {\n\tcase tea.WindowSizeMsg:\n\t\td.width = msg.Width\n\t\td.height = msg.Height\n\t\td.updateLayout()\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\n\tcase tea.KeyMsg:\n\t\t// Handle lookback picker mode\n\t\tif d.lookbackMode {\n\t\t\treturn d.handleLookbackKey(msg)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch msg.String() {\n\t\tcase \"q\", \"ctrl+c\":\n\t\t\treturn d, tea.Quit\n\t\tcase \"r\":\n\t\t\treturn d, d.collectMetrics()\n\t\tcase \"h\":\n\t\t\t// Cycle through help modes: 0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 0\n\t\t\td.helpMode = (d.helpMode + 1) % 4\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\tcase \"l\", \"L\":\n\t\t\t// Open lookback picker\n\t\t\td.lookbackMode = true\n\t\t\td.helpMode = 0 // Close help if open\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\tcase \"u\", \"U\":\n\t\t\t// Perform update if available\n\t\t\tif d.updateInfo != nil && d.updateInfo.UpdateAvailable && !d.updating {\n\t\t\t\td.updating = true\n\t\t\t\td.updateStatus = \"Downloading update...\"\n\t\t\t\treturn d, d.performUpdate()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase tickMsg:\n\t\treturn d, tea.Batch(d.tick(), d.collectMetrics(), d.checkForUpdates())\n\n\tcase metricsMsg:\n\t\td.systemMetrics = msg.system\n\t\td.tokenMetrics = msg.tokens\n\t\td.tmuxMetrics = msg.tmux\n\t\td.lastUpdate = time.Now()\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\n\tcase updateCheckMsg:\n\t\td.updateInfo = msg.info\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\n\tcase updateCompleteMsg:\n\t\td.updating = false\n\t\tif msg.err != nil {\n\t\t\td.updateStatus = fmt.Sprintf(\"Update failed: %v\", msg.err)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\td.updateStatus = \"Update complete! Restarting...\"\n\t\t\t// The app should restart automatically\n\t\t\treturn d, tea.Quit\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\n\tcase errMsg:\n\t\td.err = msg.err\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn d, nil\n}\n\n// performUpdate returns a command that applies the update\nfunc (d *Dashboard) performUpdate() tea.Cmd {\n\treturn func() tea.Msg {\n\t\terr := d.updater.PerformUpdateWithRestart(d.updateInfo)\n\t\treturn updateCompleteMsg{err: err}\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleLookbackKey handles keyboard input when lookback picker is open\nfunc (d *Dashboard) handleLookbackKey(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {\n\tif d.lookbackCustomMode {\n\t\t// Custom date/time editing mode\n\t\tswitch msg.String() {\n\t\tcase \"esc\":\n\t\t\td.lookbackCustomMode = false\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\tcase \"enter\":\n\t\t\t// Apply custom date and close picker\n\t\t\td.tokenCollector.SetLookback(d.lookbackCustomDate)\n\t\t\td.lookbackCustomMode = false\n\t\t\td.lookbackMode = false\n\t\t\treturn d, d.collectMetrics()\n\t\tcase \"tab\", \"right\":\n\t\t\td.lookbackEditField = (d.lookbackEditField + 1) % 5\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\tcase \"shift+tab\", \"left\":\n\t\t\td.lookbackEditField = (d.lookbackEditField + 4) % 5\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\tcase \"up\":\n\t\t\td.adjustCustomDate(1)\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\tcase \"down\":\n\t\t\td.adjustCustomDate(-1)\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Preset selection mode\n\tswitch msg.String() {\n\tcase \"esc\", \"l\", \"q\":\n\t\td.lookbackMode = false\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\tcase \"up\", \"k\":\n\t\tif d.lookbackSelectedIndex > 0 {\n\t\t\td.lookbackSelectedIndex--\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\tcase \"down\", \"j\":\n\t\tif d.lookbackSelectedIndex < len(d.lookbackPresets)-1 {\n\t\t\td.lookbackSelectedIndex++\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn d, nil\n\tcase \"enter\", \" \":\n\t\tpreset := d.lookbackPresets[d.lookbackSelectedIndex]\n\t\tif preset.GetTime == nil {\n\t\t\t// Custom mode - enter custom date picker\n\t\t\td.lookbackCustomMode = true\n\t\t\td.lookbackEditField = 0\n\t\t\treturn d, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Apply preset and close picker\n\t\td.tokenCollector.SetLookback(preset.GetTime())\n\t\td.lookbackMode = false\n\t\treturn d, d.collectMetrics()\n\t}\n\treturn d, nil\n}\n\n// adjustCustomDate adjusts the custom date based on current edit field\nfunc (d *Dashboard) adjustCustomDate(delta int) {\n\tswitch d.lookbackEditField {\n\tcase 0: // Year\n\t\td.lookbackCustomDate = d.lookbackCustomDate.AddDate(delta, 0, 0)\n\tcase 1: // Month\n\t\td.lookbackCustomDate = d.lookbackCustomDate.AddDate(0, delta, 0)\n\tcase 2: // Day\n\t\td.lookbackCustomDate = d.lookbackCustomDate.AddDate(0, 0, delta)\n\tcase 3: // Hour\n\t\td.lookbackCustomDate = d.lookbackCustomDate.Add(time.Duration(delta) * time.Hour)\n\tcase 4: // Minute\n\t\td.lookbackCustomDate = d.lookbackCustomDate.Add(time.Duration(delta) * time.Minute)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clamp to not be in the future\n\tif d.lookbackCustomDate.After(time.Now()) {\n\t\td.lookbackCustomDate = time.Now()\n\t}\n}\n\n// View renders the dashboard\nfunc (d *Dashboard) View() string {\n\tif d.width == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"Initializing...\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar content string\n\n\t// Check if in lookback picker mode\n\tif d.lookbackMode {\n\t\tcontent = d.renderLookbackPicker()\n\t} else if d.helpMode > 0 {\n\t\t// Check if in help mode\n\t\tcontent = d.renderHelpView()\n\t} else {\n\t\tswitch d.layoutMode {\n\t\tcase LayoutUltraWide:\n\t\t\tcontent = d.renderUltraWide()\n\t\tcase LayoutWide:\n\t\t\tcontent = d.renderWide()\n\t\tcase LayoutCompact:\n\t\t\tcontent = d.renderCompact()\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tcontent = d.renderNarrow()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add status bar\n\tstatusBar := d.renderStatusBar()\n\n\t// Join content and status bar\n\toutput := lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, content, statusBar)\n\n\t// CRITICAL: Ensure output fills the entire terminal height to prevent\n\t// external process output (like Tailscale logs) from bleeding through\n\t// at the bottom of the screen. Without this, the alternate screen buffer\n\t// may show underlying terminal content in unfilled rows.\n\toutputHeight := lipgloss.Height(output)\n\tif outputHeight < d.height {\n\t\t// Pad with empty lines to fill the screen\n\t\tpadding := strings.Repeat(\"\\n\", d.height-outputHeight-1)\n\t\toutput = output + padding\n\t}\n\n\treturn output\n}\n\n// updateLayout determines the current layout mode based on terminal size\nfunc (d *Dashboard) updateLayout() {\n\tif d.width < 120 {\n\t\t// Compact stacked layout: tmux top, tokens middle, system bottom\n\t\td.layoutMode = LayoutCompact\n\t} else if d.width < 180 {\n\t\t// Wide layout: 4 panels in 2x2 grid\n\t\td.layoutMode = LayoutWide\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Ultra-wide layout: 4 panels side-by-side\n\t\td.layoutMode = LayoutUltraWide\n\t}\n}\n\n// tick returns a command that sends a tick message every 2 seconds\nfunc (d *Dashboard) tick() tea.Cmd {\n\treturn tea.Tick(2*time.Second, func(t time.Time) tea.Msg {\n\t\treturn tickMsg(t)\n\t})\n}\n\n// metricsMsg carries collected metrics\ntype metricsMsg struct {\n\tsystem metrics.SystemMetrics\n\ttokens *metrics.TokenMetrics\n\ttmux   *metrics.TmuxMetrics\n}\n\n// errMsg carries errors\ntype errMsg struct {\n\terr error\n}\n\n// Metric type constants for cache keys\nconst (\n\tmetricTypeSystem = \"system\"\n\tmetricTypeTmux   = \"tmux\"\n)\n\n// collectMetrics returns a command that collects all metrics\n// Uses leader election: only one instance collects, others read from cache\nfunc (d *Dashboard) collectMetrics() tea.Cmd {\n\treturn func() tea.Msg {\n\t\tcache := d.tokenCollector.GetCache()\n\t\tisLeader := cache.TryAcquireLease(d.instanceID)\n\n\t\tvar system metrics.SystemMetrics\n\t\tvar tokens *metrics.TokenMetrics\n\t\tvar tmux *metrics.TmuxMetrics\n\n\t\t// Use channels to collect results with timeout\n\t\ttype systemResult struct {\n\t\t\tmetrics metrics.SystemMetrics\n\t\t}\n\t\ttype tokenResult struct {\n\t\t\tmetrics *metrics.TokenMetrics\n\t\t}\n\t\ttype tmuxResult struct {\n\t\t\tmetrics *metrics.TmuxMetrics\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsystemChan := make(chan systemResult, 1)\n\t\ttokenChan := make(chan tokenResult, 1)\n\t\ttmuxChan := make(chan tmuxResult, 1)\n\n\t\t// System metrics: try cache first, collect if leader or cache miss\n\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\tif !isLeader {\n\t\t\t\tif data, ok := cache.GetCachedMetrics(metricTypeSystem); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tvar cached metrics.SystemMetrics\n\t\t\t\t\tif json.Unmarshal(data, &cached) == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tsystemChan <- systemResult{metrics: cached}\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Leader or cache miss: collect fresh\n\t\t\tm := d.systemCollector.Collect()\n\t\t\tif isLeader {\n\t\t\t\tif data, err := json.Marshal(m); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcache.SetCachedMetrics(metricTypeSystem, data)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsystemChan <- systemResult{metrics: m}\n\t\t}()\n\n\t\t// Token metrics: always collect (uses shared DB, cheap to query)\n\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\tt, _ := d.tokenCollector.Collect()\n\t\t\ttokenChan <- tokenResult{metrics: t}\n\t\t}()\n\n\t\t// Tmux metrics: try cache first, collect if leader or cache miss\n\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\tif !isLeader {\n\t\t\t\tif data, ok := cache.GetCachedMetrics(metricTypeTmux); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tvar cached metrics.TmuxMetrics\n\t\t\t\t\tif json.Unmarshal(data, &cached) == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttmuxChan <- tmuxResult{metrics: &cached}\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Leader or cache miss: collect fresh\n\t\t\tm := d.tmuxCollector.Collect()\n\t\t\tif isLeader {\n\t\t\t\tif data, err := json.Marshal(m); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcache.SetCachedMetrics(metricTypeTmux, data)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttmuxChan <- tmuxResult{metrics: m}\n\t\t}()\n\n\t\t// Wait for results with 3 second timeout\n\t\ttimeout := time.After(3 * time.Second)\n\n\t\t// Collect results as they come in, or timeout\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < 3; i++ {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase r := <-systemChan:\n\t\t\t\tsystem = r.metrics\n\t\t\tcase r := <-tokenChan:\n\t\t\t\ttokens = r.metrics\n\t\t\tcase r := <-tmuxChan:\n\t\t\t\ttmux = r.metrics\n\t\t\tcase <-timeout:\n\t\t\t\t// Return whatever we have so far\n\t\t\t\treturn metricsMsg{\n\t\t\t\t\tsystem: system,\n\t\t\t\t\ttokens: tokens,\n\t\t\t\t\ttmux:   tmux,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn metricsMsg{\n\t\t\tsystem: system,\n\t\t\ttokens: tokens,\n\t\t\ttmux:   tmux,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// calculateTokenPanelWidth determines the optimal width for the token panel\n// based on actual content needs, returning the minimum width that displays well\nfunc (d *Dashboard) calculateTokenPanelWidth() int {\n\t// Base minimum for the panel structure (stacked layout)\n\tminWidth := 46\n\n\t// If no token metrics or not available, use minimum\n\tif d.tokenMetrics == nil || !d.tokenMetrics.Available {\n\t\treturn minWidth\n\t}\n\n\tmodelCount := len(d.tokenMetrics.ModelUsages)\n\n\t// Side-by-side layout needs:\n\t// - Left column: 22 chars (stats)\n\t// - Separator: 2 chars\n\t// - Right column: ~24 chars (model name + cost)\n\t// - Borders/padding: 4 chars\n\t// Total: ~52 chars minimum for side-by-side\n\tif modelCount > 0 {\n\t\t// With models, use side-by-side layout\n\t\t// More models = need slightly more height but same width\n\t\treturn 54\n\t}\n\n\t// Just basic token counts, no models - use narrow layout\n\treturn minWidth\n}\n\n// calculateTmuxPanelWidth determines the width needed for the TMUX panel\n// based on session count and available height (to calculate columns needed)\nfunc (d *Dashboard) calculateTmuxPanelWidth(panelHeight int) int {\n\tconst minCellWidth = 28 // Minimum readable session cell\n\tconst minWidth = 50     // Absolute minimum for 1 column\n\tconst maxWidth = 120    // Cap to prevent TMUX from taking too much space\n\n\t// If no tmux metrics, use minimum\n\tif d.tmuxMetrics == nil || !d.tmuxMetrics.Available {\n\t\treturn minWidth\n\t}\n\n\tsessionCount := len(d.tmuxMetrics.Sessions)\n\tif sessionCount == 0 {\n\t\treturn minWidth\n\t}\n\n\t// Available lines for sessions: height - title(1) - borders(2)\n\tavailableLines := panelHeight - 3\n\tif availableLines < 1 {\n\t\tavailableLines = 1\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate columns needed to fit all sessions\n\tcols := 1\n\tif sessionCount > availableLines {\n\t\tcols = (sessionCount + availableLines - 1) / availableLines // ceil division\n\t}\n\tif cols > 4 {\n\t\tcols = 4 // Reasonable maximum for readability\n\t}\n\n\t// Width needed: columns * cellWidth + separators + borders/padding\n\t// contentWidth = cols * cellWidth + (cols - 1) for separators\n\t// panelWidth = contentWidth + 4 for borders/padding\n\tneededWidth := cols*minCellWidth + (cols - 1) + 4\n\n\tif neededWidth < minWidth {\n\t\treturn minWidth\n\t}\n\tif neededWidth > maxWidth {\n\t\treturn maxWidth\n\t}\n\treturn neededWidth\n}\n\n// getTmuxColumnCount returns the number of columns needed to display all sessions\nfunc (d *Dashboard) getTmuxColumnCount(panelHeight int) int {\n\tif d.tmuxMetrics == nil || !d.tmuxMetrics.Available {\n\t\treturn 1\n\t}\n\n\tsessionCount := len(d.tmuxMetrics.Sessions)\n\tif sessionCount == 0 {\n\t\treturn 1\n\t}\n\n\tavailableLines := panelHeight - 3\n\tif availableLines < 1 {\n\t\tavailableLines = 1\n\t}\n\n\tcols := 1\n\tif sessionCount > availableLines {\n\t\tcols = (sessionCount + availableLines - 1) / availableLines\n\t}\n\tif cols > 4 {\n\t\tcols = 4\n\t}\n\treturn cols\n}\n\n// renderUltraWide renders 3 panels side-by-side\n// Balances space between Token and TMUX panels based on content needs\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderUltraWide() string {\n\t// Account for panel padding (0,1) which adds 2 chars per panel = 6 total\n\ttotalPanelWidth := d.width - 6\n\tpanelHeight := d.height - 3 // -2 borders, -1 status line\n\n\t// Step 1: System panel gets fixed width for CPU bars\n\tsystemWidth := 60\n\tif totalPanelWidth < 180 {\n\t\tsystemWidth = 55\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Calculate minimum and ideal widths for both panels\n\tminTokenWidth := 46\n\tminTmuxWidth := d.calculateTmuxPanelWidth(panelHeight)\n\n\t// Ideal token width: side-by-side layout with comfortable model display\n\t// Left column (22) + separator (2) + right column for models\n\t// Right column needs: model name (up to 15) + cost (8) + tokens (12) = ~35\n\t// Total ideal: 22 + 2 + 35 + 4 (borders) = 60\n\tidealTokenWidth := 60\n\n\t// Ideal tmux width: minimum cell width + some padding for session names\n\t// Use 35 chars per cell (28 min + 7 for longer names)\n\tidealCellWidth := 35\n\ttmuxCols := d.getTmuxColumnCount(panelHeight)\n\tidealTmuxWidth := tmuxCols*idealCellWidth + (tmuxCols - 1) + 4\n\n\t// Available space after system panel\n\tavailableWidth := totalPanelWidth - systemWidth\n\n\t// Start with minimums\n\ttokenWidth := minTokenWidth\n\ttmuxWidth := minTmuxWidth\n\n\t// Calculate how much each panel wants beyond minimum\n\ttokenWant := idealTokenWidth - minTokenWidth\n\tif tokenWant < 0 {\n\t\ttokenWant = 0\n\t}\n\ttmuxWant := idealTmuxWidth - minTmuxWidth\n\tif tmuxWant < 0 {\n\t\ttmuxWant = 0\n\t}\n\n\t// Distribute remaining space proportionally based on wants\n\tremainingAfterMins := availableWidth - minTokenWidth - minTmuxWidth\n\tif remainingAfterMins > 0 {\n\t\ttotalWant := tokenWant + tmuxWant\n\t\tif totalWant > 0 {\n\t\t\t// Proportional allocation\n\t\t\ttokenExtra := remainingAfterMins * tokenWant / totalWant\n\t\t\ttmuxExtra := remainingAfterMins - tokenExtra\n\t\t\ttokenWidth = minTokenWidth + tokenExtra\n\t\t\ttmuxWidth = minTmuxWidth + tmuxExtra\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Neither wants more, split evenly\n\t\t\ttokenWidth = minTokenWidth + remainingAfterMins/2\n\t\t\ttmuxWidth = minTmuxWidth + remainingAfterMins - remainingAfterMins/2\n\t\t}\n\t} else if remainingAfterMins < 0 {\n\t\t// Not enough space for both minimums - compress proportionally\n\t\tdeficit := -remainingAfterMins\n\t\ttotalMin := minTokenWidth + minTmuxWidth\n\t\t// Reduce each panel proportionally to its minimum\n\t\ttokenReduction := deficit * minTokenWidth / totalMin\n\t\ttmuxReduction := deficit - tokenReduction\n\t\ttokenWidth = minTokenWidth - tokenReduction\n\t\ttmuxWidth = minTmuxWidth - tmuxReduction\n\t\t// Enforce absolute minimums\n\t\tif tokenWidth < 30 {\n\t\t\ttokenWidth = 30\n\t\t\ttmuxWidth = availableWidth - tokenWidth\n\t\t}\n\t\tif tmuxWidth < 30 {\n\t\t\ttmuxWidth = 30\n\t\t\ttokenWidth = availableWidth - tmuxWidth\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Cap token panel - it doesn't need more than ideal\n\tif tokenWidth > idealTokenWidth {\n\t\texcess := tokenWidth - idealTokenWidth\n\t\ttokenWidth = idealTokenWidth\n\t\ttmuxWidth += excess\n\t}\n\n\t// Final safety: ensure widths are positive\n\tif tokenWidth < 1 {\n\t\ttokenWidth = 1\n\t}\n\tif tmuxWidth < 1 {\n\t\ttmuxWidth = 1\n\t}\n\n\tsystemPanel := d.renderSystemPanel(systemWidth, panelHeight)\n\ttokenPanel := d.renderTokenPanel(tokenWidth, panelHeight)\n\ttmuxPanel := d.renderTmuxPanel(tmuxWidth, panelHeight)\n\n\t// Force all panels to exactly the same height using lipgloss\n\t// This ensures borders align even if content varies\n\tuniformHeight := lipgloss.NewStyle().Height(panelHeight)\n\tsystemPanel = uniformHeight.Render(systemPanel)\n\ttokenPanel = uniformHeight.Render(tokenPanel)\n\ttmuxPanel = uniformHeight.Render(tmuxPanel)\n\n\t// Join horizontally with top alignment\n\treturn lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top,\n\t\tsystemPanel,\n\t\ttokenPanel,\n\t\ttmuxPanel,\n\t)\n}\n\n// renderWide renders 2 panels on top, 1 on bottom\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderWide() string {\n\tpanelWidth := (d.width - 3) / 2 // 2 panels with spacing\n\ttopHeight := (d.height - 4) / 2 // Split height\n\tbottomHeight := d.height - topHeight - 4\n\n\tsystemPanel := d.renderSystemPanel(panelWidth, topHeight)\n\ttokenPanel := d.renderTokenPanel(panelWidth, topHeight)\n\ttmuxPanel := d.renderTmuxPanel(d.width-2, bottomHeight)\n\n\ttopRow := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, systemPanel, \" \", tokenPanel)\n\n\treturn lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, topRow, tmuxPanel)\n}\n\n// renderNarrow renders panels stacked vertically\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderNarrow() string {\n\tpanelWidth := d.width - 2\n\tpanelHeight := (d.height - 5) / 3 // 3 panels stacked\n\n\tsystemPanel := d.renderSystemPanel(panelWidth, panelHeight)\n\ttokenPanel := d.renderTokenPanel(panelWidth, panelHeight)\n\ttmuxPanel := d.renderTmuxPanel(panelWidth, panelHeight)\n\n\treturn lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,\n\t\tsystemPanel,\n\t\ttokenPanel,\n\t\ttmuxPanel,\n\t)\n}\n\n// renderCompact renders panels stacked vertically for narrow terminals (e.g. 75x68):\n// tmux sessions on top, token usage in the middle, system resources on the bottom.\n// Height is distributed with tmux getting extra rows since session lists are tall.\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderCompact() string {\n\tpanelWidth := d.width - 2\n\tavailable := d.height - 8 // 3×2 border rows + 1 status line + 1 repo/updater line\n\n\t// Give tmux a bit more room — sessions need more lines than the other panels\n\ttmuxHeight := available / 2\n\tremaining := available - tmuxHeight\n\ttokenHeight := remaining / 2\n\tsystemHeight := remaining - tokenHeight\n\n\t// Enforce a sensible minimum so panels don't collapse\n\tif tmuxHeight < 8 {\n\t\ttmuxHeight = 8\n\t}\n\tif tokenHeight < 8 {\n\t\ttokenHeight = 8\n\t}\n\tif systemHeight < 8 {\n\t\tsystemHeight = 8\n\t}\n\n\ttmuxPanel := d.renderTmuxPanel(panelWidth, tmuxHeight)\n\ttokenPanel := d.renderTokenPanel(panelWidth, tokenHeight)\n\tsystemPanel := d.renderSystemPanel(panelWidth, systemHeight)\n\n\treturn lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,\n\t\ttmuxPanel,\n\t\ttokenPanel,\n\t\tsystemPanel,\n\t)\n}\n\n// renderSystemPanel renders the system resources panel\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderSystemPanel(width, height int) string {\n\tstyle := panelStyle\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Title (with emoji like unified-dashboard)\n\tlines = append(lines, successStyle.Render(\"⚡ System Resources\"))\n\n\t// Load average\n\tif d.systemMetrics.Load.Error == nil {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Load: %.2f %.2f %.2f\",\n\t\t\td.systemMetrics.Load.Load1,\n\t\t\td.systemMetrics.Load.Load5,\n\t\t\td.systemMetrics.Load.Load15))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Load: N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate content width (panel width minus borders and padding)\n\tcontentWidth := width - 4 // -2 for borders, -2 for padding\n\n\t// CPU Total - use same calculation method as Mem/Swap for consistent bar width\n\tif d.systemMetrics.CPU.Error == nil {\n\t\t// Format: \"CPU [|||||| XX.X%]\" - \"CPU \" is 4 chars\n\t\tcpuBarWidth := contentWidth - 4 // Subtract \"CPU \" prefix\n\t\tif cpuBarWidth < 10 {\n\t\t\tcpuBarWidth = 10\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"CPU %s\", d.renderBar(d.systemMetrics.CPU.TotalPercent, cpuBarWidth)))\n\n\t\t// CPU per-core - use up to 6 lines for CPU display\n\t\tmaxCoreLines := 6\n\t\ttotalCores := len(d.systemMetrics.CPU.PerCore)\n\n\t\t// Determine label width based on total cores (for alignment)\n\t\tlabelWidth := 1\n\t\tif totalCores >= 100 {\n\t\t\tlabelWidth = 3\n\t\t} else if totalCores >= 10 {\n\t\t\tlabelWidth = 2\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Determine cores per line\n\t\tvar coresPerLine int\n\t\tif totalCores <= 6 {\n\t\t\tcoresPerLine = 1 // One core per line - bars stretch full width\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Multiple cores per line - calculate how many fit\n\t\t\t// Each core needs: labelWidth + \":[\" + barContent + \"]\" + space\n\t\t\t// Minimum reasonable bar content is about 12 chars\n\t\t\tminCharsPerCore := labelWidth + 3 + 12 // label + \":[]\" + min bar\n\t\t\tcoresPerLine = contentWidth / minCharsPerCore\n\t\t\tif coresPerLine < 2 {\n\t\t\t\tcoresPerLine = 2 // At least 2 per line when splitting\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Calculate bar width for cores - align brackets by using fixed widths\n\t\tvar barWidth int\n\t\tif coresPerLine == 1 {\n\t\t\t// Single core per line - match memory/swap calculation for consistency\n\t\t\t// Format: \"NN:[||||... XXX%]\"\n\t\t\t// labelWidth + \":[]\" = labelWidth + 3 chars overhead\n\t\t\tbarWidth = contentWidth - labelWidth - 3\n\t\t\tif barWidth < 10 {\n\t\t\t\tbarWidth = 10\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Multiple cores per line - split width evenly\n\t\t\t// Account for spaces between cores (1 space separator)\n\t\t\tspacesBetween := coresPerLine - 1\n\t\t\twidthPerCore := (contentWidth - spacesBetween) / coresPerLine\n\t\t\t// Subtract label overhead: labelWidth + \":[]\"\n\t\t\tbarWidth = widthPerCore - labelWidth - 3\n\t\t\tif barWidth < 8 {\n\t\t\t\tbarWidth = 8\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Max cores we can display with 6 lines\n\t\tmaxDisplayCores := coresPerLine * maxCoreLines\n\t\tmaxCores := totalCores\n\t\tif maxCores > maxDisplayCores {\n\t\t\tmaxCores = maxDisplayCores\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar coreLine strings.Builder\n\t\tlinesUsed := 0\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < maxCores; i++ {\n\t\t\tif i > 0 && i%coresPerLine == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// Start new line\n\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, coreLine.String())\n\t\t\t\tcoreLine.Reset()\n\t\t\t\tlinesUsed++\n\t\t\t\tif linesUsed >= maxCoreLines {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif coreLine.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tcoreLine.WriteString(\" \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Render progress bar for this core with calculated width\n\t\t\t// Use consistent label width for alignment - brackets align because\n\t\t\t// we use fixed-width labels and fixed-width bar content\n\t\t\tpercent := d.systemMetrics.CPU.PerCore[i]\n\t\t\tminiBar := d.renderMiniBar(percent, barWidth)\n\t\t\tcoreLine.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%*d:[%s]\", labelWidth, i, miniBar))\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Add remaining cores on current line\n\t\tif coreLine.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, coreLine.String())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif totalCores > maxCores {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"+%d more cores\", totalCores-maxCores)))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"CPU: N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Memory - always compact (one line)\n\tif d.systemMetrics.Memory.Error == nil {\n\t\tmemUsed := metrics.FormatBytes(d.systemMetrics.Memory.Used)\n\t\tmemTotal := metrics.FormatBytes(d.systemMetrics.Memory.Total)\n\t\t// Format: \"Mem [||||...] XX.XX GB/XX.XX GB\"\n\t\t// Calculate bar width: contentWidth - \"Mem \" (4) - \" \" (1) - \"used/total\" - margins\n\t\tbarWidth := contentWidth - 5 - len(memUsed) - 1 - len(memTotal)\n\t\tif barWidth < 10 {\n\t\t\tbarWidth = 10\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Mem %s %s/%s\",\n\t\t\td.renderBar(d.systemMetrics.Memory.Percentage, barWidth),\n\t\t\tmemUsed, memTotal))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Mem: N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Swap - always compact (one line)\n\tif d.systemMetrics.Swap.Error == nil && d.systemMetrics.Swap.Total > 0 {\n\t\tswpUsed := metrics.FormatBytes(d.systemMetrics.Swap.Used)\n\t\tswpTotal := metrics.FormatBytes(d.systemMetrics.Swap.Total)\n\t\t// Use same calculation as Memory for consistency\n\t\tbarWidth := contentWidth - 5 - len(swpUsed) - 1 - len(swpTotal)\n\t\tif barWidth < 10 {\n\t\t\tbarWidth = 10\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Swp %s %s/%s\",\n\t\t\td.renderBar(d.systemMetrics.Swap.Percentage, barWidth),\n\t\t\tswpUsed, swpTotal))\n\t}\n\n\t// Disk Usage - always compact (one line)\n\tif d.systemMetrics.DiskUsage.Error == nil {\n\t\tdiskUsed := metrics.FormatBytes(d.systemMetrics.DiskUsage.Used)\n\t\tdiskTotal := metrics.FormatBytes(d.systemMetrics.DiskUsage.Total)\n\t\t// Use same calculation as Memory for consistency\n\t\tbarWidth := contentWidth - 5 - len(diskUsed) - 1 - len(diskTotal)\n\t\tif barWidth < 10 {\n\t\t\tbarWidth = 10\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Dsk %s %s/%s\",\n\t\t\td.renderBar(d.systemMetrics.DiskUsage.Percentage, barWidth),\n\t\t\tdiskUsed, diskTotal))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Dsk: N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Disk I/O - verbose format with pipe separators\n\tif d.systemMetrics.DiskIO.Error == nil {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Disk I/O | Read: %s | Write: %s\",\n\t\t\tmetrics.FormatRate(d.systemMetrics.DiskIO.ReadBytesPerSec),\n\t\t\tmetrics.FormatRate(d.systemMetrics.DiskIO.WriteBytesPerSec)))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Disk I/O | N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Net I/O - verbose format with pipe separators\n\tif d.systemMetrics.NetIO.Error == nil {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Net I/O  | Recv: %s | Sent: %s\",\n\t\t\tmetrics.FormatRate(d.systemMetrics.NetIO.RecvBytesPerSec),\n\t\t\tmetrics.FormatRate(d.systemMetrics.NetIO.SentBytesPerSec)))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Net I/O  | N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n}\n\n// renderNetworkPanel renders network I/O metrics with per-interface breakdown\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderNetworkPanel(width, height int) string {\n\tstyle := panelStyle\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Title\n\tlines = append(lines, successStyle.Render(\"📡 Network I/O\"))\n\n\tcontentWidth := width - 4 // Account for borders and padding\n\n\t// Show aggregate rates if available\n\tif d.systemMetrics.NetIO.Error == nil {\n\t\t// Calculate width for rate bars - leave room for labels and values\n\t\t// Format: \"Recv [||||...] XX.XX KB/s\"\n\t\tmaxRateWidth := 12 // \"XXX.XX KB/s\"\n\t\trateBarWidth := contentWidth - 6 - maxRateWidth // \"Recv \" + bar + \" rate\"\n\t\tif rateBarWidth < 8 {\n\t\t\trateBarWidth = 8\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Normalize rates for bar display (cap at 10 MB/s for full bar)\n\t\tconst maxDisplayRate = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10 MB/s\n\t\trecvPercent := (d.systemMetrics.NetIO.RecvBytesPerSec / maxDisplayRate) * 100\n\t\tsentPercent := (d.systemMetrics.NetIO.SentBytesPerSec / maxDisplayRate) * 100\n\t\tif recvPercent > 100 {\n\t\t\trecvPercent = 100\n\t\t}\n\t\tif sentPercent > 100 {\n\t\t\tsentPercent = 100\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trecvRate := metrics.FormatRate(d.systemMetrics.NetIO.RecvBytesPerSec)\n\t\tsentRate := metrics.FormatRate(d.systemMetrics.NetIO.SentBytesPerSec)\n\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Recv %s %s\",\n\t\t\td.renderBar(recvPercent, rateBarWidth), recvRate))\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Sent %s %s\",\n\t\t\td.renderBar(sentPercent, rateBarWidth), sentRate))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Aggregate: N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Separator\n\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\n\t// Per-interface breakdown\n\tif d.systemMetrics.NetIO.Error == nil && len(d.systemMetrics.NetIO.Interfaces) > 0 {\n\t\t// Calculate column widths for alignment\n\t\t// Format: \"eth0: ↓ XX.XX KB/s  ↑ YY.YY KB/s  | Total: ↓ ZZ.ZZ GB  ↑ WW.WW GB\"\n\t\tconst ifaceNameWidth = 8 // Max interface name width\n\t\tconst rateWidth = 10    // \"XX.XX KB/s\"\n\t\tconst totalWidth = 10   // \"ZZ.ZZ GB\"\n\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(\"Interface              Rate                Total\"))\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(\"─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\"))\n\n\t\tfor _, iface := range d.systemMetrics.NetIO.Interfaces {\n\t\t\trecvRate := metrics.FormatRate(iface.RecvBytesPerSec)\n\t\t\tsentRate := metrics.FormatRate(iface.SentBytesPerSec)\n\t\t\ttotalRecv := metrics.FormatBytes(iface.TotalRecvBytes)\n\t\t\ttotalSent := metrics.FormatBytes(iface.TotalSentBytes)\n\n\t\t\t// Format: \"ifaceName: ↓ rate  ↑ rate  | ↓ total  ↑ total\"\n\t\t\t// Using unicode arrows for direction indicators\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%-*s ↓ %-*s ↑ %-*s  | ↓ %-*s ↑ %s\",\n\t\t\t\tifaceNameWidth, iface.Name,\n\t\t\t\trateWidth, recvRate,\n\t\t\t\trateWidth, sentRate,\n\t\t\t\ttotalWidth, totalRecv,\n\t\t\t\ttotalSent))\n\t\t}\n\t} else if d.systemMetrics.NetIO.Error == nil {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(\"No active interfaces\"))\n\t} else {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Interfaces: N/A\"))\n\t}\n\n\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n}\n\n// renderTokenPanel renders the token usage panel with side-by-side layout\n// Left side: Total token stats, Right side: Per-model costs\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderTokenPanel(width, height int) string {\n\tstyle := panelStyle\n\n\tif d.tokenMetrics == nil {\n\t\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(\"Loading token metrics...\")\n\t}\n\n\tcontentWidth := width - 4 // Account for borders and padding\n\n\t// Title with lookback info aligned right\n\ttitle := successStyle.Render(\"💰 Token Usage\")\n\tlookbackInfo := \"\"\n\tif d.tokenMetrics != nil && !d.tokenMetrics.LookbackFrom.IsZero() {\n\t\t// Format start time - use date if not this week\n\t\tnow := time.Now()\n\t\tstartTime := d.tokenMetrics.LookbackFrom\n\t\telapsed := now.Sub(startTime)\n\n\t\t// Choose format based on how long ago\n\t\tvar timeStr string\n\t\tif elapsed < 7*24*time.Hour {\n\t\t\ttimeStr = startTime.Format(\"Mon 3:04pm\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeStr = startTime.Format(\"Jan 2 3:04pm\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add human-readable duration\n\t\tdurationStr := metrics.FormatDuration(elapsed)\n\t\tlookbackInfo = dimStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s → Now (%s)\", timeStr, durationStr))\n\t} else if d.tokenMetrics != nil {\n\t\tlookbackInfo = dimStyle.Render(\"All time\")\n\t}\n\n\ttitleLen := lipgloss.Width(title)\n\tlookbackLen := lipgloss.Width(lookbackInfo)\n\tspacing := contentWidth - titleLen - lookbackLen\n\tif spacing < 1 {\n\t\tspacing = 1\n\t}\n\theaderLine := title + strings.Repeat(\" \", spacing) + lookbackInfo\n\n\tif !d.tokenMetrics.Available {\n\t\tvar lines []string\n\t\tlines = append(lines, headerLine)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Not Available\"))\n\t\tif d.tokenMetrics.Error != \"\" {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, wrapText(d.tokenMetrics.Error, width-4))\n\t\t}\n\t\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\t\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n\t}\n\n\t// Helper to get model style by name\n\tgetModelStyle := func(modelName string) lipgloss.Style {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(modelName, \"opus\") {\n\t\t\treturn lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(\"#ff6b6b\")) // Red for Opus\n\t\t} else if strings.Contains(modelName, \"sonnet\") {\n\t\t\treturn lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(\"#4ecdc4\")) // Cyan for Sonnet\n\t\t} else if strings.Contains(modelName, \"haiku\") {\n\t\t\treturn lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(\"#95e1d3\")) // Light green for Haiku\n\t\t} else if strings.Contains(modelName, \"glm\") {\n\t\t\treturn lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(\"#00bfff\")) // Blue for GLM\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn dimStyle\n\t}\n\n\t// Build left column: Total stats\n\tvar leftLines []string\n\thasCacheRead := d.tokenMetrics.CacheReadTokens > 0\n\thasCacheCreate := d.tokenMetrics.CacheCreationTokens > 0\n\thasRate := d.tokenMetrics.Rate > 0\n\thasAvg := d.tokenMetrics.SessionAvgRate > 0\n\n\t// Compact format for left column\n\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"In:    %s\", metrics.FormatTokensCompact(d.tokenMetrics.InputTokens)))\n\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Out:   %s\", metrics.FormatTokensCompact(d.tokenMetrics.OutputTokens)))\n\tif hasCacheRead {\n\t\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Cache: %s\", metrics.FormatTokensCompact(d.tokenMetrics.CacheReadTokens)))\n\t}\n\tif hasCacheCreate {\n\t\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Create:%s\", metrics.FormatTokensCompact(d.tokenMetrics.CacheCreationTokens)))\n\t}\n\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Total: %s\", boldStyle.Render(metrics.FormatTokensCompact(d.tokenMetrics.TotalTokens))))\n\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Reqs:  %d\", d.tokenMetrics.Prompts))\n\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Cost:  %s\", costStyle.Render(metrics.FormatCost(d.tokenMetrics.TotalCost))))\n\tif hasRate {\n\t\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Rate:  %s\", dimStyle.Render(metrics.FormatTokenRateCompact(d.tokenMetrics.Rate))))\n\t}\n\tif hasAvg {\n\t\tleftLines = append(leftLines, fmt.Sprintf(\"Avg:   %s\", dimStyle.Render(metrics.FormatTokenRateCompact(d.tokenMetrics.SessionAvgRate))))\n\t}\n\n\t// Determine layout based on width\n\t// For narrow panels, stack vertically; for wider panels, use side-by-side\n\tmodelCount := len(d.tokenMetrics.ModelUsages)\n\tuseSideBySide := contentWidth >= 48 && modelCount > 0\n\n\t// Calculate available width for model names based on layout\n\t// In side-by-side: rightWidth = contentWidth - leftWidth(22) - separator(2)\n\t// Model line format: \"Name $XX.XX (XX,XXXtok)\" - cost ~8 chars, tokens ~12 chars = ~20 chars for cost+tokens\n\tleftWidth := 22\n\trightWidth := contentWidth - leftWidth - 2\n\tmaxModelNameWidth := rightWidth - 22 // Reserve space for cost and token count\n\tif maxModelNameWidth < 10 {\n\t\tmaxModelNameWidth = 10 // Minimum display width\n\t}\n\n\t// Build right column: Per-model costs with dynamic name width\n\tvar rightLines []string\n\tif modelCount > 0 {\n\t\trightLines = append(rightLines, boldStyle.Render(\"Models:\"))\n\t\tfor _, usage := range d.tokenMetrics.ModelUsages {\n\t\t\tdisplayName := shortenModelName(usage.Model)\n\t\t\t// Dynamically truncate based on available space\n\t\t\tif len(displayName) > maxModelNameWidth {\n\t\t\t\tdisplayName = displayName[:maxModelNameWidth-1] + \"…\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmodelStyle := getModelStyle(usage.Model)\n\t\t\t// All model info on one line: Name Cost (Tokens)\n\t\t\tline := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\",\n\t\t\t\tmodelStyle.Render(displayName),\n\t\t\t\tcostStyle.Render(metrics.FormatCost(usage.Cost)),\n\t\t\t\tdimStyle.Render(\"(\"+metrics.FormatTokensCompact(usage.TotalTokens)+\")\"))\n\t\t\trightLines = append(rightLines, line)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tlines = append(lines, headerLine)\n\n\tif useSideBySide {\n\t\t// Side-by-side: left column for totals, right for models\n\n\t\t// Pad columns to equal height\n\t\tmaxLines := len(leftLines)\n\t\tif len(rightLines) > maxLines {\n\t\t\tmaxLines = len(rightLines)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor len(leftLines) < maxLines {\n\t\t\tleftLines = append(leftLines, \"\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor len(rightLines) < maxLines {\n\t\t\trightLines = append(rightLines, \"\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Combine columns\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < maxLines; i++ {\n\t\t\tleft := leftLines[i]\n\t\t\tright := rightLines[i]\n\t\t\t// Pad left to fixed width\n\t\t\tleftPadded := left + strings.Repeat(\" \", max(0, leftWidth-lipgloss.Width(left)))\n\t\t\t// Truncate right if needed (safety fallback)\n\t\t\tif lipgloss.Width(right) > rightWidth {\n\t\t\t\tright = right[:rightWidth-1] + \"…\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, leftPadded+\"│ \"+right)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Stacked layout for narrow panels\n\t\tlines = append(lines, leftLines...)\n\t\tif modelCount > 0 {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, rightLines...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n}\n\n// shortenModelName shortens common model names for display\nfunc shortenModelName(name string) string {\n\t// Common patterns to shorten\n\treplacements := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"claude-opus-4-5-20251101\":    \"Opus 4.5\",\n\t\t\"claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929\":  \"Sonnet 4.5\",\n\t\t\"claude-haiku-4-5-20250929\":   \"Haiku 4.5\",\n\t\t\"claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022\":  \"Sonnet 3.5\",\n\t\t\"claude-3-5-haiku-20241022\":   \"Haiku 3.5\",\n\t\t\"claude-3-opus-20240229\":      \"Opus 3\",\n\t\t\"claude-3-sonnet-20240229\":    \"Sonnet 3\",\n\t\t\"claude-3-haiku-20240307\":     \"Haiku 3\",\n\t\t// GLM models (Zhipu AI)\n\t\t\"glm-4-alltools\":                \"GLM 4\",\n\t\t\"glm-4-9b-chat\":                \"GLM 4\",\n\t\t\"glm-4-air\":                     \"GLM 4\",\n\t\t\"glm-4-flash\":                   \"GLM 4\",\n\t\t\"glm-4-plus\":                    \"GLM 4+\",\n\t}\n\n\tif short, ok := replacements[name]; ok {\n\t\treturn short\n\t}\n\n\t// Try GLM partial matches\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"glm-4\") {\n\t\treturn \"GLM 4\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"glm-3\") {\n\t\treturn \"GLM 3\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Try Claude partial matches\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"opus-4-5\") || strings.Contains(name, \"opus-4.5\") {\n\t\treturn \"Opus 4.5\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"sonnet-4-5\") || strings.Contains(name, \"sonnet-4.5\") {\n\t\treturn \"Sonnet 4.5\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"haiku-4-5\") || strings.Contains(name, \"haiku-4.5\") {\n\t\treturn \"Haiku 4.5\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"opus\") {\n\t\treturn \"Opus\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"sonnet\") {\n\t\treturn \"Sonnet\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(name, \"haiku\") {\n\t\treturn \"Haiku\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn name\n}\n\n// calculateRequiredTokenWidth calculates the minimum width needed for the token panel\n// based on the longest model name in the current data\nfunc (d *Dashboard) calculateRequiredTokenWidth() int {\n\t// Base width needed for: \"  Name: $XXX.XX (X,XXX,XXX tok)\"\n\t// 2 (indent) + name + 2 (\": \") + 8 (cost) + 2 (\" (\") + 11 (tokens) + 5 (\" tok)\") + 6 (borders/padding)\n\tconst baseWidth = 36 // Everything except model name\n\n\tmaxNameLen := 10 // Default minimum for \"Sonnet 4.5\"\n\n\tif d.tokenMetrics != nil && len(d.tokenMetrics.ModelUsages) > 0 {\n\t\tfor _, usage := range d.tokenMetrics.ModelUsages {\n\t\t\tdisplayName := shortenModelName(usage.Model)\n\t\t\tif len(displayName) > maxNameLen {\n\t\t\t\tmaxNameLen = len(displayName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn baseWidth + maxNameLen\n}\n\n// renderTmuxPanel renders the tmux sessions panel\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderTmuxPanel(width, height int) string {\n\tstyle := panelStyle\n\n\tif d.tmuxMetrics == nil {\n\t\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(\"Loading tmux metrics...\")\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Calculate content width for right-alignment\n\tcontentWidth := width - 4 // Account for borders and padding\n\n\t// Count sessions by status\n\tstatusCounts := make(map[metrics.SessionStatus]int)\n\tfor _, session := range d.tmuxMetrics.Sessions {\n\t\tstatusCounts[session.Status]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build status summary (right-justified)\n\tvar statusParts []string\n\tif count := statusCounts[metrics.StatusWorking]; count > 0 {\n\t\tstatusParts = append(statusParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"🟢%d\", count))\n\t}\n\tif count := statusCounts[metrics.StatusReady]; count > 0 {\n\t\tstatusParts = append(statusParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"🔴%d\", count))\n\t}\n\tif count := statusCounts[metrics.StatusActive]; count > 0 {\n\t\tstatusParts = append(statusParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"🟡%d\", count))\n\t}\n\tif count := statusCounts[metrics.StatusError]; count > 0 {\n\t\tstatusParts = append(statusParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"❌%d\", count))\n\t}\n\tstatusSummary := strings.Join(statusParts, \" \")\n\n\t// Title with total count and status summary right-justified\n\t// Show source indicator: 🔗 for hooks, 📺 for tmux\n\tsourceIcon := \"📺\"\n\tsourceLabel := \"Sessions\"\n\tif d.tmuxMetrics.Source == \"hooks\" {\n\t\tsourceIcon = \"🔗\"\n\t\tsourceLabel = \"Sessions\"\n\t} else if d.tmuxMetrics.HooksInstalled && !d.tmuxMetrics.HooksAvailable {\n\t\t// Hooks installed but no hook sessions, falling back to tmux\n\t\tsourceLabel = \"Sessions (tmux)\"\n\t}\n\t// Show process count if different from tracked sessions (indicates detection gap)\n\tcountStr := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", d.tmuxMetrics.Total)\n\tif d.tmuxMetrics.RunningProcesses > 0 && d.tmuxMetrics.RunningProcesses != d.tmuxMetrics.Total {\n\t\tcountStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d/%d procs\", d.tmuxMetrics.Total, d.tmuxMetrics.RunningProcesses)\n\t}\n\ttitle := successStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s (%s)\", sourceIcon, sourceLabel, countStr))\n\ttitleLen := lipgloss.Width(title)\n\tsummaryLen := lipgloss.Width(statusSummary)\n\tspacing := contentWidth - titleLen - summaryLen\n\tif spacing < 1 {\n\t\tspacing = 1\n\t}\n\n\theaderLine := title + strings.Repeat(\" \", spacing) + statusSummary\n\tlines = append(lines, headerLine)\n\n\tif !d.tmuxMetrics.Available {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, errorStyle.Render(\"Not Available\"))\n\t\tif d.tmuxMetrics.Error != \"\" {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, wrapText(d.tmuxMetrics.Error, width-4))\n\t\t}\n\t\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\t\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(d.tmuxMetrics.Sessions) == 0 {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, \"No active sessions\")\n\t\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\t\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate available lines for sessions\n\t// height includes borders, subtract: title(1) + borders(2) = 3 lines overhead\n\tavailableLines := height - 3\n\tif availableLines < 1 {\n\t\tavailableLines = 1\n\t}\n\n\tsessionCount := len(d.tmuxMetrics.Sessions)\n\tcontentWidth = width - 4 // -4 for borders (2) and padding (2)\n\n\t// Calculate columns needed to show ALL sessions (priority: show everything)\n\tminCellWidth := 28  // Minimum readable session cell\n\tmaxCellWidth := 55  // Maximum cell width to avoid excessive whitespace\n\tcols := 1\n\tif sessionCount > availableLines {\n\t\t// Calculate columns needed to fit all sessions\n\t\tcols = (sessionCount + availableLines - 1) / availableLines // ceil division\n\t}\n\n\t// Cap columns based on available width\n\tmaxCols := contentWidth / minCellWidth\n\tif maxCols < 1 {\n\t\tmaxCols = 1\n\t}\n\tif cols > maxCols {\n\t\tcols = maxCols\n\t}\n\tif cols > 4 {\n\t\tcols = 4 // Reasonable maximum for readability\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate cell width based on actual columns used, but cap to avoid whitespace.\n\t// Only add columns for aesthetic reasons (reducing cell width) when we're already\n\t// in multi-column mode due to session count — never force multiple columns purely\n\t// for width when sessions fit comfortably in one column.\n\tcellWidth := (contentWidth - (cols - 1)) / cols\n\tif cols > 1 && cellWidth > maxCellWidth {\n\t\t// Increase columns to reduce cell width\n\t\toptimalCols := (contentWidth + maxCellWidth - 1) / maxCellWidth\n\t\tif optimalCols > cols && optimalCols <= 4 {\n\t\t\tcols = optimalCols\n\t\t\tcellWidth = (contentWidth - (cols - 1)) / cols\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Can't add more columns, just cap the width\n\t\t\tcellWidth = maxCellWidth\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Show ALL sessions - calculate how many we can actually display\n\tmaxDisplayed := availableLines * cols\n\tmaxSessions := sessionCount\n\tif maxSessions > maxDisplayed {\n\t\tmaxSessions = maxDisplayed // Only limit if we truly can't fit more\n\t}\n\n\t// Render sessions in vertical columns (fill first column, then second, etc.)\n\trowCount := (maxSessions + cols - 1) / cols\n\tfor row := 0; row < rowCount; row++ {\n\t\tvar rowCells []string\n\t\tfor col := 0; col < cols; col++ {\n\t\t\tidx := col*rowCount + row\n\t\t\tif idx < maxSessions {\n\t\t\t\tsession := d.tmuxMetrics.Sessions[idx]\n\t\t\t\tcellContent := d.renderSessionCell(session, cellWidth)\n\t\t\t\t// Apply explicit width constraint using lipgloss\n\t\t\t\tcellStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(cellWidth)\n\t\t\t\tcell := cellStyle.Render(cellContent)\n\t\t\t\trowCells = append(rowCells, cell)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Empty cell for alignment\n\t\t\t\temptyCell := lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(cellWidth).Render(\"\")\n\t\t\t\trowCells = append(rowCells, emptyCell)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Join cells with space separator for multiple columns\n\t\tseparator := \"\"\n\t\tif cols > 1 {\n\t\t\tseparator = \" \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(rowCells) == 1 {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, rowCells[0])\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, strings.Join(rowCells, separator))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Show \"... and X more\" if sessions were limited\n\tif maxSessions < sessionCount {\n\t\tremaining := sessionCount - maxSessions\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"... +%d more\", remaining)))\n\t}\n\n\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\treturn style.Width(width).Height(height).Render(content)\n}\n\n// renderSessionCell renders a single tmux session cell\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderSessionCell(session metrics.TmuxSession, width int) string {\n\temoji := session.Status.GetEmoji()\n\n\t// Convert ANSI color codes to hex colors for lipgloss\n\tcolorMap := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"\\033[32m\": \"#00ff00\", // Green - WORKING (Claude processing)\n\t\t\"\\033[31m\": \"#ff0000\", // Red - READY (Waiting for input)\n\t\t\"\\033[33m\": \"#ffff00\", // Yellow - ACTIVE (User in session)\n\t\t\"\\033[91m\": \"#ff5555\", // Bright Red - ERROR (Error state)\n\t\t\"\\033[0m\":  \"#ffffff\", // White/Reset\n\t}\n\n\tansiColor := session.Status.GetColor()\n\tcolor, ok := colorMap[ansiColor]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ffffff\"\n\t}\n\n\tstatusStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(color))\n\n\tattached := \"\"\n\tattachedWidth := 0\n\tif session.Attached {\n\t\tattached = \"📎\"\n\t\tattachedWidth = 3 // emoji + space\n\t}\n\n\t// Format: emoji name status windows idle attached\n\tstatusText := string(session.Status)\n\tif len(statusText) > 7 {\n\t\tstatusText = statusText[:7]\n\t}\n\n\t// Format idle duration\n\tidleStr := \"\"\n\tif session.IdleDuration > 0 {\n\t\tif session.IdleDuration < time.Minute {\n\t\t\tidleStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%ds\", int(session.IdleDuration.Seconds()))\n\t\t} else if session.IdleDuration < time.Hour {\n\t\t\tidleStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%dm\", int(session.IdleDuration.Minutes()))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tidleStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%dh\", int(session.IdleDuration.Hours()))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate available width for session name\n\t// Fixed parts: emoji(2) + space(1) + space(1) + status(7) + space(1) + windows(~3) + space(1) + idle(3) + space(1) + attached\n\t// Fixed overhead = ~20 chars + attachedWidth\n\tfixedOverhead := 20 + attachedWidth\n\tmaxNameLen := width - fixedOverhead\n\tif maxNameLen < 6 {\n\t\tmaxNameLen = 6 // Minimum readable name length\n\t}\n\n\tname := session.Name\n\tif len(name) > maxNameLen {\n\t\tname = name[:maxNameLen-1] + \"…\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the line with dynamic name width\n\tnameFormat := fmt.Sprintf(\"%%-%ds\", maxNameLen)\n\tline := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \"+nameFormat+\" %s %dw %-3s %s\",\n\t\temoji,\n\t\tname,\n\t\tstatusStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"%-7s\", statusText)),\n\t\tsession.Windows,\n\t\tidleStr,\n\t\tattached)\n\n\treturn line\n}\n\n// renderLookbackPicker renders the lookback time picker overlay\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderLookbackPicker() string {\n\tpanelHeight := d.height - 3\n\tpanelWidth := 60\n\tif panelWidth > d.width-4 {\n\t\tpanelWidth = d.width - 4\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Title\n\tlines = append(lines, boldStyle.Render(\"📅 Token Usage Lookback\"))\n\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\n\tif d.lookbackCustomMode {\n\t\t// Custom date/time picker\n\t\tlines = append(lines, \"Set custom start date/time:\")\n\t\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\n\t\t// Date/time fields\n\t\tyear := d.lookbackCustomDate.Year()\n\t\tmonth := int(d.lookbackCustomDate.Month())\n\t\tday := d.lookbackCustomDate.Day()\n\t\thour := d.lookbackCustomDate.Hour()\n\t\tminute := d.lookbackCustomDate.Minute()\n\n\t\t// Highlight selected field\n\t\tfieldStyle := dimStyle\n\t\tselectedStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\t\tBackground(lipgloss.Color(\"#00aaff\")).\n\t\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#000000\")).\n\t\t\tBold(true)\n\n\t\tyearStr := fmt.Sprintf(\" %04d \", year)\n\t\tmonthStr := fmt.Sprintf(\" %02d \", month)\n\t\tdayStr := fmt.Sprintf(\" %02d \", day)\n\t\thourStr := fmt.Sprintf(\" %02d \", hour)\n\t\tminStr := fmt.Sprintf(\" %02d \", minute)\n\n\t\tif d.lookbackEditField == 0 {\n\t\t\tyearStr = selectedStyle.Render(yearStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tyearStr = fieldStyle.Render(yearStr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif d.lookbackEditField == 1 {\n\t\t\tmonthStr = selectedStyle.Render(monthStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmonthStr = fieldStyle.Render(monthStr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif d.lookbackEditField == 2 {\n\t\t\tdayStr = selectedStyle.Render(dayStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdayStr = fieldStyle.Render(dayStr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif d.lookbackEditField == 3 {\n\t\t\thourStr = selectedStyle.Render(hourStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\thourStr = fieldStyle.Render(hourStr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif d.lookbackEditField == 4 {\n\t\t\tminStr = selectedStyle.Render(minStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tminStr = fieldStyle.Render(minStr)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  Date: %s-%s-%s\", yearStr, monthStr, dayStr))\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  Time: %s:%s\", hourStr, minStr))\n\t\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(\"  ↑/↓: adjust value  ←/→/Tab: change field\"))\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(\"  Enter: apply  Esc: back to presets\"))\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Preset selection\n\t\t// Calculate available content lines: panelHeight - 4 (borders + padding)\n\t\tavailableLines := panelHeight - 4\n\t\t// Compact mode needed if: title(1) + instruction(1) + presets(7*2=14) + nav(1) = 17 > available\n\t\t// Use compact single-line format when height is constrained\n\t\tcompactMode := availableLines < 17\n\n\t\tlines = append(lines, \"Select a lookback period:\")\n\t\tif !compactMode {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor i, preset := range d.lookbackPresets {\n\t\t\tprefix := \"  \"\n\t\t\tstyle := dimStyle\n\t\t\tif i == d.lookbackSelectedIndex {\n\t\t\t\tprefix = \"▶ \"\n\t\t\t\tstyle = successStyle\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Show time for presets with GetTime\n\t\t\ttimeStr := \"\"\n\t\t\tif preset.GetTime != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt := preset.GetTime()\n\t\t\t\tif !t.IsZero() {\n\t\t\t\t\ttimeStr = fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", t.Format(\"Jan 2 3:04pm\"))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif compactMode {\n\t\t\t\t// Single-line compact format: \"▶ Name - Description (time)\"\n\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s %s%s\",\n\t\t\t\t\tprefix,\n\t\t\t\t\tstyle.Render(preset.Name),\n\t\t\t\t\tdimStyle.Render(\"- \"+preset.Description),\n\t\t\t\t\tdimStyle.Render(timeStr)))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Full two-line format\n\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s%s\",\n\t\t\t\t\tprefix,\n\t\t\t\t\tstyle.Render(preset.Name),\n\t\t\t\t\tdimStyle.Render(timeStr)))\n\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"   %s\", dimStyle.Render(preset.Description)))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !compactMode {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, dimStyle.Render(\"  ↑/↓/j/k: navigate  Enter/Space: select  Esc/l: close\"))\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the picker panel\n\tcontent := strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n\n\tpickerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tBorderStyle(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).\n\t\tBorderForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#ffaa00\")).\n\t\tPadding(1, 2).\n\t\tWidth(panelWidth).\n\t\tHeight(panelHeight)\n\n\tpicker := pickerStyle.Render(content)\n\n\t// Center the picker on screen\n\tleftPad := (d.width - panelWidth) / 2\n\tif leftPad < 0 {\n\t\tleftPad = 0\n\t}\n\n\treturn lipgloss.NewStyle().PaddingLeft(leftPad).Render(picker)\n}\n\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderHelpView() string {\n\tpanelHeight := d.height - 3\n\ttotalPanelWidth := d.width - 2 // Match normal view width calculation\n\tpanelWidth := (totalPanelWidth * 40) / 100 // 40% for panel\n\n\tvar panel string\n\tvar helpText string\n\tvar title string\n\n\tswitch d.helpMode {\n\tcase 1: // System Resources\n\t\ttitle = \"System Resources Panel\"\n\t\tpanel = d.renderSystemPanel(panelWidth, panelHeight)\n\t\thelpText = `Real-time system metrics updated every 2s:\n\nCPU: Overall + per-core usage as N:[||| XX%]\n  Colors: Green<60% Yellow60-79% Orange80-94% Red≥95%\n  ≤6 cores: one per line, >6: multiple per line\n\nMemory/Swap: Used/Total with percentage bars\n  Formatted in GB/MB for readability\n\nDisk: Root filesystem (/) usage with percentage bar\n  Shows used/total space in GB/TB\n\nDisk I/O: Read/write speeds in bytes/s or KB/s\n\nNet I/O: Network recv/sent speeds\n\nLoad: 1min, 5min, 15min averages\n  Indicates overall system activity level`\n\n\tcase 2: // Token Usage\n\t\ttitle = \"Token Usage Panel\"\n\t\tpanel = d.renderTokenPanel(panelWidth, panelHeight)\n\t\thelpText = `Tracks token usage from ~/.claude/projects (Claude) and zai-proxy metrics (GLM):\n\nTokens:\n  In/Out: Input/output tokens\n  Cache Read/Create: Cache operations (Claude only)\n  Total: All tokens combined\n  Cost: Estimated API cost ($)\n\nRates:\n  Rate: Current tok/min (60s window)\n  Avg: Session average tok/min\n\nTime:\n  Span: Duration of lookback period\n  Active: Time since first activity in period\n\nLookback: Press 'l' to open time picker\n  Presets: Today, 24h, 7d, 30d, All time\n  Custom: Set specific date/time with arrows\n\nModels: Per-model cost breakdown\n  Color-coded: Opus(red) Sonnet(cyan) Haiku(green) GLM(blue)\n  Sorted by cost (highest first)\n\nData Sources:\n  - Claude: ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl (sessions)\n  - GLM: Not currently tracked (zai-proxy only exports Prometheus)\n\nSQLite Cache: .ccdash/tokens.db\n  Queryable with DuckDB or any SQLite tool\n  Tables: token_events, file_state\n  Incremental ingestion with deduplication`\n\n\tcase 3: // TMUX Sessions\n\t\ttitle = \"TMUX Sessions Panel\"\n\t\tpanel = d.renderTmuxPanel(panelWidth, panelHeight)\n\t\thelpText = `Monitors tmux sessions running Claude Code:\n\nTitle: Shows total count + status summary\n  Format: \"📺 TMUX Sessions (N) 🟢2 🔴1\"\n\nStatus (analyzes pane content):\n  🟢 WORKING - Claude Code processing\n  🔴 READY - Waiting for user input\n  🟡 ACTIVE - User in session\n  ❌ ERROR - Error or undefined state\n\nDetection: Analyzes last 15 lines for:\n  Working indicators, prompts, errors\n\nSession Info:\n  Name, status, windows (Xw), idle, 📎=attached\n\nIdle: Time since content changed (s/m/h)\n\nLayout: Auto-columns based on count/width\n\nSelf-Update: Press 'u' when update available\n  Status bar shows \"⬆ vX.X.X available!\"`\n\t}\n\n\t// Create help text panel with wrapping that preserves line breaks\n\thelpWidth := d.width - panelWidth - 6 // Remaining width for help text\n\tif helpWidth < 40 {\n\t\thelpWidth = 40\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate available lines for help text\n\t// panelHeight includes borders (2 lines), padding, title (1 line), blank line (1 line)\n\tavailableLines := panelHeight - 4 // -4 for borders, title, and spacing\n\tif availableLines < 5 {\n\t\tavailableLines = 5\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap the help text\n\twrappedHelp := wrapTextPreserveBreaks(helpText, helpWidth-4)\n\thelpLines := strings.Split(wrappedHelp, \"\\n\")\n\n\t// Check if we need 2-column layout\n\tvar finalHelpText string\n\tif len(helpLines) > availableLines {\n\t\t// Use 2-column layout to fit more content\n\t\tcolumnWidth := (helpWidth - 6) / 2 // Split into 2 columns with spacing\n\n\t\t// Re-wrap text to narrower column width first\n\t\twrappedForColumns := wrapTextPreserveBreaks(helpText, columnWidth-2)\n\t\tcolumnLines := strings.Split(wrappedForColumns, \"\\n\")\n\n\t\t// Split lines into two columns at midpoint\n\t\tmidPoint := (len(columnLines) + 1) / 2\n\n\t\t// Ensure we don't exceed available lines\n\t\tif midPoint > availableLines {\n\t\t\tmidPoint = availableLines\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tleftLines := columnLines[:midPoint]\n\t\tvar rightLines []string\n\t\tif midPoint < len(columnLines) {\n\t\t\tendPoint := midPoint * 2\n\t\t\tif endPoint > len(columnLines) {\n\t\t\t\tendPoint = len(columnLines)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trightLines = columnLines[midPoint:endPoint]\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Pad shorter column with empty lines for alignment\n\t\tfor len(rightLines) < len(leftLines) {\n\t\t\trightLines = append(rightLines, \"\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Ensure both columns fit within available lines\n\t\tif len(leftLines) > availableLines {\n\t\t\tleftLines = leftLines[:availableLines]\n\t\t\trightLines = rightLines[:availableLines]\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Build columns with fixed width\n\t\tvar leftCol, rightCol strings.Builder\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(leftLines); i++ {\n\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tleftCol.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t\t\trightCol.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tleftCol.WriteString(leftLines[i])\n\t\t\tif i < len(rightLines) {\n\t\t\t\trightCol.WriteString(rightLines[i])\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tleftColPanel := lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(columnWidth).Render(leftCol.String())\n\t\trightColPanel := lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(columnWidth).Render(rightCol.String())\n\n\t\tfinalHelpText = lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, leftColPanel, \"  \", rightColPanel)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Single column is fine\n\t\tfinalHelpText = strings.Join(helpLines, \"\\n\")\n\t}\n\n\thelpPanel := lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tBorderStyle(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).\n\t\tBorderForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#ffaa00\")). // Orange for help\n\t\tPadding(0, 1).\n\t\tWidth(helpWidth).\n\t\tHeight(panelHeight).\n\t\tRender(successStyle.Render(title) + \"\\n\\n\" + finalHelpText)\n\n\t// Render panel on left, help on right\n\treturn lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, panel, \" \", helpPanel)\n}\n\n// renderStatusBar renders the status bar at the bottom of the view.\n//\n// Wide/ultrawide (≥120 cols): single line —\n//   time+version  |  repo link or update notice  |  dimensions+shortcuts\n//\n// Compact (<120 cols): two lines —\n//   Line 1: repo link (always) or update notice\n//   Line 2: time+version on the left, dimensions+shortcuts on the right\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderStatusBar() string {\n\tleft := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s\", d.lastUpdate.Format(\"15:04:05\"), d.version)\n\n\tshortcuts := \"l:lookback h:help q:quit r:refresh\"\n\tif d.updateInfo != nil && d.updateInfo.UpdateAvailable && !d.updating {\n\t\tshortcuts = \"u:update l:lookback h:help q:quit r:refresh\"\n\t}\n\tright := fmt.Sprintf(\"%dx%d %s\", d.width, d.height, shortcuts)\n\n\t// Build the repo/update middle string\n\tvar middle string\n\tif d.updating {\n\t\tmiddle = warningStyle.Render(d.updateStatus)\n\t} else if d.updateStatus != \"\" {\n\t\tmiddle = errorStyle.Render(d.updateStatus)\n\t} else if d.updateInfo != nil && d.updateInfo.UpdateAvailable {\n\t\tmiddle = successStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"⬆ %s available! Press u to update\", d.updateInfo.LatestVersion))\n\t} else {\n\t\tmiddle = dimStyle.Render(\"https://github.com/jedarden/ccdash\")\n\t}\n\n\tif d.layoutMode != LayoutCompact {\n\t\t// Wide / ultrawide: single line with repo link centred between left and right\n\t\ttotalContent := lipgloss.Width(left) + lipgloss.Width(middle) + lipgloss.Width(right)\n\t\tavailableSpace := d.width - totalContent - 2 // -2 for statusBarStyle padding\n\t\tif availableSpace < 4 {\n\t\t\t// Not enough room — drop the middle\n\t\t\tcompactShortcuts := \"l h q r\"\n\t\t\tif d.updateInfo != nil && d.updateInfo.UpdateAvailable {\n\t\t\t\tcompactShortcuts = \"u l h q r\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn statusBarStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %dx%d %s\",\n\t\t\t\td.lastUpdate.Format(\"15:04\"), d.version, d.width, d.height, compactShortcuts))\n\t\t}\n\t\tleftSpacer := strings.Repeat(\" \", availableSpace/2)\n\t\trightSpacer := strings.Repeat(\" \", availableSpace-availableSpace/2)\n\t\treturn statusBarStyle.Render(left + leftSpacer + middle + rightSpacer + right)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compact: two lines so the repo link is always visible alongside the shortcuts\n\ttopWidth := lipgloss.Width(middle)\n\ttopPad := max(0, (d.width-topWidth)/2)\n\ttopLine := strings.Repeat(\" \", topPad) + middle\n\n\ttotalContent := lipgloss.Width(left) + lipgloss.Width(right)\n\tavailableSpace := d.width - totalContent - 2\n\tvar statusLine string\n\tif availableSpace < 2 {\n\t\tcompactShortcuts := \"h q r\"\n\t\tif d.updateInfo != nil && d.updateInfo.UpdateAvailable {\n\t\t\tcompactShortcuts = \"u h q r\"\n\t\t}\n\t\tstatusLine = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %dx%d %s\",\n\t\t\td.lastUpdate.Format(\"15:04\"), d.version, d.width, d.height, compactShortcuts)\n\t} else {\n\t\tstatusLine = left + strings.Repeat(\" \", availableSpace) + right\n\t}\n\n\treturn lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,\n\t\ttopLine,\n\t\tstatusBarStyle.Render(statusLine),\n\t)\n}\n\n// renderBar renders a progress bar with percentage inside (unified-dashboard style)\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderBar(percent float64, width int) string {\n\tif width < 10 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Determine color based on threshold (unified-dashboard style)\n\tvar color string\n\tif percent >= 95 {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ff0000\" // Red\n\t} else if percent >= 80 {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ffaa00\" // Orange\n\t} else if percent >= 60 {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ffff00\" // Yellow\n\t} else {\n\t\tcolor = \"#00ff00\" // Green\n\t}\n\n\t// Format percentage\n\tpercentText := fmt.Sprintf(\"%.1f%%\", percent)\n\n\t// Calculate fill width (accounting for percentage text)\n\tbarWidth := width - 2 // Account for brackets\n\tavailableWidth := barWidth - len(percentText) - 1 // -1 for space before %\n\tfillWidth := int(percent / 100.0 * float64(availableWidth))\n\tif fillWidth > availableWidth {\n\t\tfillWidth = availableWidth\n\t}\n\tif fillWidth < 0 {\n\t\tfillWidth = 0\n\t}\n\n\t// Create filled and empty portions (vertical bar style like unified-dashboard)\n\tfilled := strings.Repeat(\"|\", fillWidth)\n\tempty := strings.Repeat(\" \", availableWidth-fillWidth)\n\n\t// Apply styling\n\tbarStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(color))\n\tdimStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(\"#888888\"))\n\n\tvar bar strings.Builder\n\tbar.WriteString(\"[\")\n\tbar.WriteString(barStyle.Render(filled))\n\tbar.WriteString(dimStyle.Render(empty))\n\tbar.WriteString(\" \")\n\tbar.WriteString(percentText)\n\tbar.WriteString(\"]\")\n\n\treturn bar.String()\n}\n\n// renderMiniBar creates a compact progress bar with percentage inside\n// Format: \"||| 42%\" for use in CPU core display\n// Returns a fixed-width string to ensure bracket alignment\nfunc (d *Dashboard) renderMiniBar(percent float64, barWidth int) string {\n\t// Determine color based on threshold\n\tvar color string\n\tif percent >= 95 {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ff0000\" // Red\n\t} else if percent >= 80 {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ffaa00\" // Orange\n\t} else if percent >= 60 {\n\t\tcolor = \"#ffff00\" // Yellow\n\t} else {\n\t\tcolor = \"#00ff00\" // Green\n\t}\n\n\t// Format percentage with fixed width (4 chars: \"XXX%\" or \" XX%\")\n\tpercentText := fmt.Sprintf(\"%3.0f%%\", percent)\n\n\t// Reserve space for percentage text (4 chars) + 1 space\n\tpercentSpace := 5\n\tbarAvailableWidth := barWidth - percentSpace\n\tif barAvailableWidth < 1 {\n\t\tbarAvailableWidth = 1\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate fill width\n\tfillWidth := int(percent / 100.0 * float64(barAvailableWidth))\n\tif fillWidth > barAvailableWidth {\n\t\tfillWidth = barAvailableWidth\n\t}\n\tif fillWidth < 0 {\n\t\tfillWidth = 0\n\t}\n\n\t// Create filled and empty portions\n\tfilled := strings.Repeat(\"|\", fillWidth)\n\tempty := strings.Repeat(\" \", barAvailableWidth-fillWidth)\n\n\t// Apply styling\n\tbarStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(color))\n\tdimStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(\"#888888\"))\n\n\treturn barStyle.Render(filled) + dimStyle.Render(empty) + \" \" + percentText\n}\n\n// Styles (unified-dashboard palette)\nvar (\n\tpanelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tBorderStyle(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).\n\t\tBorderForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#00aaff\")).\n\t\tPadding(0, 1) // Minimal padding for compact display\n\n\ttitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tBold(true).\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#00ffff\")).\n\t\tMarginBottom(1)\n\n\tboldStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tBold(true)\n\n\tsuccessStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#00ff00\"))\n\n\tcostStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#ffaa00\")).\n\t\tBold(true)\n\n\twarningStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#ffaa00\"))\n\n\terrorStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#ff0000\")).\n\t\tBold(true)\n\n\tdimStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#888888\"))\n\n\tstatusBarStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().\n\t\tForeground(lipgloss.Color(\"#00ffff\")).\n\t\tBackground(lipgloss.Color(\"#1a1a1a\")).\n\t\tPadding(0, 1)\n)\n\n// Utility functions\n\nfunc max(a, b int) int {\n\tif a > b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\nfunc wrapText(text string, width int) string {\n\tif len(text) <= width {\n\t\treturn text\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\twords := strings.Fields(text)\n\tcurrentLine := \"\"\n\n\tfor _, word := range words {\n\t\tif len(currentLine)+len(word)+1 <= width {\n\t\t\tif currentLine == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcurrentLine = word\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tcurrentLine += \" \" + word\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tif currentLine != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, currentLine)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcurrentLine = word\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif currentLine != \"\" {\n\t\tlines = append(lines, currentLine)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// wrapTextPreserveBreaks wraps text while preserving explicit line breaks\nfunc wrapTextPreserveBreaks(text string, width int) string {\n\t// Split by newlines to preserve paragraph structure\n\tparagraphs := strings.Split(text, \"\\n\")\n\tvar wrappedParagraphs []string\n\n\tfor _, para := range paragraphs {\n\t\tpara = strings.TrimSpace(para)\n\t\tif para == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Preserve empty lines\n\t\t\twrappedParagraphs = append(wrappedParagraphs, \"\")\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Wrap this paragraph\n\t\tif len(para) <= width {\n\t\t\twrappedParagraphs = append(wrappedParagraphs, para)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Word wrap this line\n\t\twords := strings.Fields(para)\n\t\tcurrentLine := \"\"\n\t\tfor _, word := range words {\n\t\t\tif currentLine == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcurrentLine = word\n\t\t\t} else if len(currentLine)+len(word)+1 <= width {\n\t\t\t\tcurrentLine += \" \" + word\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\twrappedParagraphs = append(wrappedParagraphs, currentLine)\n\t\t\t\tcurrentLine = word\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif currentLine != \"\" {\n\t\t\twrappedParagraphs = append(wrappedParagraphs, currentLine)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(wrappedParagraphs, \"\\n\")\n}\n\nfunc formatDuration(d time.Duration) string {\n\tif d < time.Minute {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.0fs\", d.Seconds())\n\t} else if d < time.Hour {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.0fm\", d.Minutes())\n\t} else if d < 24*time.Hour {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.1fh\", d.Hours())\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.1fd\",
